item.title: Re: Pianist feedback
item.content:encoded: I think it's rather odd that the user is referred to as -A, but it still works. +1

item.title: Re: Pianist feedback
item.content:encoded:
Hm… I admit I'm no piano expert, so the technical specifications you've provided are more than welcome. Thanks!

Oh, and also:

It seems like the implication of this section is that a person would instantly be struck by a mess of conflicting memories.

That's exactly the implication I was going for, and that also reflects at the testing logs, where no D-class experienced either happy or sad memories, but a mixture of the two.

item.title: Pianist feedback
item.content:encoded:
Hey! I'm a pretty infrequent Foundation user, but I'm a professional pianist. I'm commenting to suggest some edits to tone. A lot of my stuff is nitpicky, but I think it could help immersion if you implemented it, so I hope you will consider it.

+ show block
– hide block

SCP-4562 is a black Bösendorfer concert grand piano.

Can I suggest that you make it specifically a Bösendorfer Model 290 "Imperial"? This is because the article image depicts an Imperial, and because the Imperial has 9 more keys than standard, which seems relevant to your anomalous effects.

Low-note keys … High-note keys … Black keys

I don't think I 100% understand how this can work. When I play almost anything, I usually have to use white keys and black keys across the range, without preference. It seems like the implication of this section is that a person would instantly be struck by a mess of conflicting memories.

You define a melody as

Any coherent set of notes that together form a music piece.

How do you define 'coherent'? Many pieces of modern classical music would not be considered analytical in terms of common practice music theory.

A white envelope with the words "My Music" written on its front side

How large is this envelope? I ask because you mentioned that Beethoven's 'Ode to Joy' is in it. The 'Ode' movement is about 24 minutes long; that's a lot of pages, in any format. Speaking of which -

the other 22 were famous music pieces such as Beethoven's Ode to Joy.

1) I would suggest not saying that it's the Ode, but rather 'a reduction of [the Ode] for solo piano'. This is because I think it's unlikely that anyone is playing from the unreduced orchestral score, but the ambiguity exists in the article as written.

2) I wouldn't reference it solely as the 'Ode to Joy'; technically, that's the poem. Even 'Beethoven's Ode to Joy' could mean "another composer's setting of Beethoven's 1803 version of the text". I would recommend referencing it as 'the 4th movement of Ludwig van Beethoven's Symphony No. 9, Op. 125 (the "Ode to Joy")'.

Chopin's Funeral March

I would recommend updating this to 'Frederic Chopin's Piano Sonata No. 2 in B-flat minor, Op. 35: III. Marche funebre - Lento'

item.title: Author post or author spam or something
item.content:encoded: What can I say… Rising from -5 to +9 is a good thing. At least this is not faced with deletion no more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I gave you +1, because I liked the concept and it looks well polished. Granted I did only skim through the article

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Lol, neither do I…
Thanks for commenting here, I am really glad to see that people like my idea!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I dont understand why this is in the negatives…
Anyway nice skip +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you very much for taking the time to review this!

I fixed the repetitive phrase in the description, it was indeed something that was bothering me too, and as for the testing logs, I started rewriting the whole Addendum as most of the reviewers found some issues on the logs.

really like how in the last log the unstable guy got unstable brainwaves from the piano. Favorite bit.

Hey, thank you man. I am very glad you liked this, the last log entry was the most difficult one to write, I am happy it payed out!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In the description, the final sentence of the last bullet-point and following sentence in the next paragraph both begin with "It should be noted that…"; you should rephrase one of the two.

For the logs, some details are irrelevant and should be taken out. Being Caucasian for example, since this didn't affect any of the subjects.

Otherwise, what Drelic said.

And also, really like how in the last log the unstable guy got unstable brainwaves from the piano. Favorite bit.

item.title: Re: novote
item.content:encoded: Hmm… It seems like the test log is the issue most of the reviewers have. I will start rewriting it and hopefully it will get better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, the test logs were the primary problem I had during the Draft phase. When I started writing, I had around 6 - 7 entries, and I reduced them to the 4 that are now.
As for the content, the reason it is so condensed is because I tried to make the emotional stages of each D-class during the piece they were playing very clear, as well as their reactions to it.
However, I will try to reduce the content a bit-it indeed seems like an issue.

item.title: Re: novote
item.content:encoded: I agree, concept is great it's just the logs that need a little bit of a rewrite to have a more clinical tone.

item.title: novote
item.content:encoded: I don't really like how the clinical tone in the log descriptions are, and it seems like an essay to me. I love the concept, but truthfully when I got to the logs I was fairly disappointed. I love the idea and description, but the logs just lack a little clinical tone and interest. I'll be an upvote when the logs fix even a little

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The logs are too condensed for me to read. Have you tried reducing the overall content of the logs?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oops! Fixed it, thank you for mentioning it .

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: the only problem I see is that in piece 12/Ludwig van Beethoven's ode to joy is that there is a SCP-XXXX instead of the number.

item.title: Author post
item.content:encoded:
Oof….after, like, 1 month of polishing, getting reviews, updating and rewriting, my new skip is finally here! A simple story about the connection between life and music, and how the simplest things can affect the outcome…

I would like to thank these people for their help:

  • cybersqyd (concept review, first greenlight, draft review, writing help via PMs and a final look on the draft)
  • RockTeethMothEyes (concept review, second greenlight)
  • Neutrino (concept review via IRC)
  • PrLosash (draft review)
  • Nagiros (concept review)
  • AnomalousDucky (concept review)
  • Zyn (taking care of a general matter during the conceptualization phase)
  • TheMightyMcB (for the above ^)
Image credit
- close

The image is CC compliant. It comes from here:
https://ccsearch.creativecommons.org/photos/828d6925-0b63-49c7-94ab-78c8c03cff8f

And now, la parole est a vous (it's your time to speak):what do you think of this idea?
